pageDescription: 6-month end-of-study internship in France. Build P78's propulsion test facility — from layout and instrumentation through hot-fire campaigns.

sections.label: Why P78
paragraphs: P78 is building the propulsion systems and orbital vehicles that connect every asset in space — from Earth orbit to the lunar surface. Interns at P78 are embedded in the team and contributing to real projects from day one.

You'll take ownership of a defined problem, work directly with the engineers building the hardware, and deliver something real. Background and institution matter less than attitude and ability. If you're curious, self-directed, and ready to learn quickly, we'd like to hear from you.

sections.label: About the role
paragraphs: You'll own the build-out of P78's propulsion test facility in France — from layout and utilities to instrumentation and initial operations. This is hands-on infrastructure work on hardware that will fire real propellants. You'll work directly with the founders and be involved across the full propulsion programme as it develops.
This is an end-of-study internship with a view to a permanent role for the right person.

sections.label: What you'd work on
bullets: Test cell design and build: layout, propellant lines, instrumentation, data acquisition
Supporting hot-fire test campaigns as the facility comes online
Contributing to propulsion system development as required

sections.label: What we're looking for
bullets: Strong mechanical or aerospace engineering fundamentals
Comfortable with hands-on hardware work — you build things, not just model them
Some exposure to fluid systems, test infrastructure, or instrumentation is a plus
Self-directed — this is a real project with real deliverables, not a structured placement
